name: psocsgdt
what_this_is: M9.5 -- SGDT (0F 01 /0) / SIDT (0F 01 /1): store the 6-byte GDTR/IDTR pseudo-descriptor to memory (the reverse of LGDT/LIDT). Used by OS/driver/memory-manager init to read the descriptor-table registers; both previously HALTed (only /2 LGDT /3 LIDT were decoded). Not privileged (any CPL).
transitions: reset (F000:FFF0) -> 16-bit real mode; cr0 stays 0x60000010 (no paging)
build + LGDT a known GDT pseudo-descriptor {limit=0x0123, base=0x00456789}; LIDT {limit=0x07FF, base=0x00001000}
read the stored bytes back into GPRs (ax/bx/cx = gdt limit/base15:0/base31:16; di/si/bp = idt) so the per-record differential sees them
implementation: decode extends the 0F 01 arm: /0 -> SYS_SGDT, /1 -> SYS_SIDT (mem form, d_mem_write), gated on !paging_on (the 2-beat S_SGDT store does not integrate page translation -> else HALT). S_SGDT mirrors S_LGDT's read layout as a WRITE: beat 0 @[mem] = {base[15:0], limit[15:0]} (wstrb 1111), beat 1 @[mem+4] = base[31:16] (wstrb 0011). SYS_SGDT selects gdt_base/gdt_limit, else idt. base[31:24]=0 in the test keeps the 16-bit (24-bit base) and 32-bit forms identical so the gate is unambiguous.
why_per_record_differential: SGDT/SIDT are synchronous, deterministic stores; the per-record compare cannot see arbitrary memory, so the firmware reads the stored bytes back into GPRs (incl. a MOV-moffs accumulator load) which the compare DOES check vs qemu over all 26 retired instructions.
non_vacuous: The gate asserts BOTH traces reach the final readback (pc=0x58, idt base31:16 -> bp). If SGDT/SIDT HALTed the trace would stop earlier and never reach it.
zero_new_rtl_note: Scoped to !paging_on (the SoC boot path). The user-mode corpus never executes SGDT/SIDT and runs with paging configurations off the decoded path, so the build is byte-identical (make verify 77/77 + verify-sys 20/20).
description: Bare-metal M9.5 SoC SGDT/SIDT differential. A real-mode -bios firmware LGDT/LIDTs known values, SGDT/SIDTs them back to memory, and reads the stored bytes into GPRs; proves the store is byte-identical to qemu-system-i386 -cpu pentium over all 26 retired instructions, per-record, with a non-vacuous final-readback-reached assertion on both traces. Completes the M9.5 boot-ISA gap set (CPUID, far CALL/RETF, LES/LDS family, SGDT/SIDT).
